step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1's assessment. Disability-adjusted life years (DALYs) indeed provide a more holistic view of the burden of disease because they take into account both the number of lives affected and the severity of those effects through adjustments for disabilities and illnesses. This approach offers policymakers valuable insights beyond just counting fatalities, making it superior as a broad indicator of overall population health impact.
